steps.text: Write the perimeter formula for a rectangle: $P = 2(\text{length} + \text{width})$. With length = 48 and width = $x$: $P = 2(48 + x) = 96 + 2x$.
"At most 140 inches" means the perimeter is less than or equal to 140, so the inequality is $2x + 96 \leq 140$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $2x + 96 \leq 140$ — matches the perimeter expression with the correct ≤ sign for "at most." That confirms A is correct.
  • B: $2x + 96 \geq 140$ — ≥ means "at least," not "at most." Eliminated.
  • C: $x + 48 \leq 140$ — omits the factor of 2; this is only half the perimeter. Eliminated.
  • D: $x + 48 \geq 140$ — wrong sign and wrong perimeter expression. Eliminated.
